style_stack.visual: broll_montage PRIMARY (matrix=static plates+hard cuts; smoke keeps Ken Burns)

beats.speakable_text: If a heavy morning is familiar, this depression pattern needs a better reset.
Trying to outrun depression usually makes the loop more expensive. More forcing, more guilt about not doing more. And less real rest.
Depression often persists because the reward system stops signaling that action will actually pay off. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.
Try this. Pick one two-minute task, do only that task. And let done count even if it's small. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.
Bookmark the pattern. Signal, reset, next action. Small honest actions count even when they don't feel like progress yet.
